candidates.direction_name: Restore The Accepted Compact PTX Cadence After The Failed Barrier Trim
ranking_notes: This is the most pragmatic next move because the current head is simply worse than the accepted round-8 base.
It clears the failed sync variant before the mandatory round-10 checkpoint and remote push.
It lets subsequent high-upside branches start from the best currently accepted PTX surface instead of a falsified one.
candidates.hypothesis: The latest wait/sync retime is a measured loss. Restoring the accepted compact PTX cadence should recover the branch baseline before additional experiments, keep the search tree honest, and avoid carrying the 8.96% barrier seam into the round-10 checkpoint and subsequent exploration.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: This is a recovery direction rather than a new bottleneck theory; its purpose is to remove a falsified sync variant and return to the accepted compact PTX surface.
candidates.risk: Low. This is a narrow restore of the previously accepted loop cadence on the active PTX branch.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if restoring the accepted cadence requires touching any code outside the compact PTX wait/sync seam that regressed in round 9.
expected_local_changes: Restore the compact two-stage wait/sync seam to the accepted round-8 cadence.
Keep grouped_rows at 4, stage depth at 2, and shared memory at 22,016 B.
Do not layer any new exploit on top of the restore in the same implementation.

candidates.direction_name: Reopen The Writer-Safe 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Branch From The Accepted PTX Base
ranking_notes: This remains the highest-ceiling structural branch once the accepted compact PTX base is restored.
The writer-safe 256x128 path already exists in the tree, so the reopen is bounded even though it is still risky.
It ranks below dir_01 only because the immediate need is to clear the known-losing sync variant before the checkpoint.
candidates.hypothesis: If the compact PTX family remains capped around 24.7 ms after the restore, the remaining headroom is probably geometric. The writer-safe 256x128 / 64x64 path is still the cleanest structural branch consistent with the persistent human guidance.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: The 128x128 PTX surface may still be constrained by geometry and warp-reuse limits once the local cadence is back on the accepted base.
candidates.risk: High. This is a broader structural reopen that touches geometry, ownership, and export path together.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the reopen requires reviving a previously broken ownership or export path instead of the writer-safe 256x128 surface already present in the source.
expected_local_changes: Re-enable the writer-safe 256x128 / 64x64 PTX hot-band branch from the accepted compact PTX base.
Keep async-copy staging and shared-memory reuse intact on the larger CTA geometry.
Leave non-hot-band routing unchanged in the first pass.

candidates.direction_name: Port The PTX Hot-Band Path To The Existing 2-K Pg2s Stage Schedule
ranking_notes: This is the next distinct pipeline family after the failed single-K barrier trim.
The code already contains a neighboring two-K-stage schedule, so the implementation has a concrete in-tree reference.
It stays rank 3 because restoring the accepted base and evaluating the structural 256x128 branch are both cleaner first steps.
candidates.hypothesis: The compact single-K PTX path still pays synchronization every K tile, and the failed barrier-trim proves the seam is fragile. Reusing the existing two-K-stage hot-band schedule as the PTX cadence reference is the next distinct pipeline family if the restored accepted base still needs more latency hiding.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier, refill cadence, and latency hiding on the dominant hot-band path rather than raw DRAM bandwidth.
candidates.risk: Moderate-high. This touches prolog, refill cadence, and drain logic together and may raise shared-memory or register pressure.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the port grows shared memory enough to worsen occupancy beyond the current 2-CTA limit or if correctness requires broader launch-family rewrites.
expected_local_changes: Reuse the existing two-K-stage hot-band schedule as the PTX refill reference.
Batch two A/B async-copy loads per stage so the PTX hot-band path synchronizes less often.
Keep the launch surface inside the current 128x128 hot-band region in the first pass.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
